channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Live: Swans set for Magpies challenge while Suns tackle Power
item.description: The ladder-leading Swans are at home to the Magpies while the Suns tackle the Power up north in a Friday night AFL double-header. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Henry Hanson and Dean Bilton

media:group.media:description: The Swans are top of the ladder coming into the round 10 clash with the Magpies.

item.title: Property developer bids $50,000 donation for lunch with deputy premier
item.description: The developer, David Trask, made the successful bid as a donation at lunch on Friday for the ruling LNP, held at the Brisbane Convention Centre and dubbed "Delivering for Queensland Industry Update".
item.dc:creator: Liam Walsh and Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Queensland Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie oversees state agency Economic Development Queensland.

item.title: 'Barbecue stopper' Asian tiger mosquito detected on Australian mainland for the first time
item.description: The world's most invasive mosquito has previously been found at borders, islands, and ports, but has just been discovered in a remote mainland Queensland community.
item.dc:creator: Adam Stephen and Conor Byrne

media:group.media:description: The female Aedes albopictus, also known as the Asian tiger mosquito.

item.title: Regional incinerator for Sydney waste draws unanimous ire at inquiry
item.description: A parliamentary hearing into a proposed $600 million waste-to-energy plant at Tarago has heard widespread opposition and condemnation from the community.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Fuller

media:group.media:description: Community members and witnesses were staunch in their opposition to the waste-to-energy plant at the parliamentary inquiry hearing.

item.title: WA treasurer concedes $217 million cost of racetrack project may blow out
item.description: Despite assurances to the contrary, WA Treasurer Rita Saffioti has conceded it would be difficult for the government to stick to the $217.5 million budget for the Perth Park project.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Withers

media:group.media:description: Rita Saffioti speaks to reporters at the Burswood park site.

item.title: Shoplifting is so bad in this town that police are honing in
item.description: In the regional Victorian town of Shepparton, shoplifting and retail crime are so rife that police have launched a dedicated patrol unit to target repeat offenders.
item.dc:creator: Charmaine Manuel and Andrew Mangelsdorf

media:group.media:description: Police have launched a new focused operation on retail theft in Shepparton.

item.title: Chloroform in drain suspected as reason hospital ward shut, staff ill
item.description: An incident that prompted a Tasmanian hospital theatre to evacuate, and left several staff feeling ill, was possibly caused by chloroform, documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Bec Pridham

media:group.media:description: According to RTI documents, it is hypothesised alcohol and chlorine were poured down the same sink, creating chloroform.

item.title: Live: 'Forgot they were playing tackle': Dogs rinsed for 'soft' start to Magic Round
item.description: One of the biggest weekends on the NRL calendar begins tonight as Magic Round kicks off in Brisbane. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Ronaldo Mulitalo is hit in the face by Connor Tracey.

item.title: Neo-Nazi group banned in Australia under hate laws
item.description: White Australia will be listed as a hate group, making it illegal to support the organisation in any way.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke says the listing takes effect from midnight.

item.title: Ali Moore to hang up headphones as 774 Melbourne Drive host
item.description: Veteran broadcaster Ali Moore will step away from the show at the end of the month, with Charlie Pickering to join the program as the new host.

media:group.media:description: After a four-decade career in broadcasting, Ali Moore is stepping down as host of 774 ABC Melbourne Drive.

item.title: More than 13 years' jail for massage therapist who sexually abused 61 women
item.description: Two women who were sexually assaulted by an unqualified massage therapist have implored women to not feel afraid to "speak up" if they're ever in the same situation, after he was sentenced to more than 13 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: Former Glenelg masseuse Sumit Satish Rastogi has been sentenced.

item.title: Government told to 'stop shelving' landmark Islamophobia report
item.description: The author of a landmark report on Islamophobia in Australia says the federal government is taking too long to act on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Nabil Al-Nashar and Niddal Mustafa

media:group.media:description: Islamaphobia envoy Aftab Malik is asking the federal government to "urgently" respond to a report he handed down last year.

item.title: Police to hold children up to 48 hours under NT youth justice changes
item.description: Northern Territory Police will be able to hold children in watch houses for up to 48 hours and question them without an adult present under planned changes to the Youth Justice Act.
item.dc:creator: Tilda Colling

media:group.media:description: Planned amendments to the NT's Youth Justice Act would mean police no longer have to release youth detainees after 24 hours if not brought before a judge.

item.title: Inquest into death of Clare Nowland hears from relatives on final day
item.description: Family of Clare Nowland have addressed a coroners court on the final day of an inquest examining her death.
item.dc:creator: Adriane Reardon and Isla Evans

media:group.media:description: Clare Nowland, who had symptoms of dementia, died in Cooma Hospital in 2023 after being tasered.

item.title: Union reaches in-principle deal giving Victorian teachers up to 32pc pay rise
item.description: A drawn out pay dispute between Victoria's government and public sector teachers is set to come to an end, with the union reaching an in-principle deal for pay rises between 28 and 32 per cent.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Brown

media:group.media:description: Teachers cheer during speeches at the strike rally in Melbourne.

item.title: Attempted murder charges laid after man shot in stomach
item.description: The man allegedly responsible for a shooting in Tasmania's north west on Wednesday has been charged with attempted murder.
item.dc:creator: Sandy Powell

media:group.media:description: Police at the scene of the shooting on Wednesday.

item.title: Three-month closure for regional and remote courthouses
item.description: Western Australia's top judges have released a public notice saying many courthouses in regional and remote areas will close for at least three months due to a lack of security.
item.dc:creator: Mietta Adams and Alistair Bates

media:group.media:description: Halls Creek Magistrates Court is one of many regional courthouses to close for three months.

item.title: Universities warn against Coalition plan to cut foreign student numbers
item.description: Universities are bracing for foreign student numbers to absorb the bulk of cuts under a Coalition migration plan, with experts warning skilled visas would be difficult to cull.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Asher

media:group.media:description: Foreign students could be targeted under the Coalition's proposed migration crackdown.

item.title: Speeding driver who killed acclaimed novelist jailed for four years
item.description: Lucas Martins Da Silva was driving his hatchback more than 30 kilometres an hour over the speed limit when he struck Brenda Walker in Perth's CBD.
item.dc:creator: David Weber

media:group.media:description: Brenda Walker was killed when she was hit by a car on Kings Park Road in December 2024.

item.title: 'Gut feeling': Rising Socceroos star on why he moved to Norwich City
item.description: Rising Socceroos striker Mo Toure says a "gut feeling" was the reason behind his wildly successful move to the English Championship.

media:group.media:description: Mo Toure's move to Championship side Norwich City has more than paid off, with the Socceroos star scoring nine times in 11 league appearances.

item.title: How likely is Delta Goodrem to bring home the Eurovision crown?
item.description: In a dazzling show of light and a powerful, tone-perfect delivery, Delta Goodrem may have just given Australia a real shot at winning Eurovision for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Annabel Bowles

media:group.media:description: Delta Goodrem has advanced to the 2026 Eurovision grand final.

item.title: Victorian ex-premier Andrews to be immortalised with $134k statue
item.description: The government says work is already underway to honour the state's longest-serving Labor premier.

media:group.media:description: A statue of long-serving premier Daniel Andrews's likeness will be installed at Treasury Place.

item.title: Coalition tax plan could cost more than suggested in first few years
item.description: The Coalition's plan to index tax brackets could cost $35 billion in the first four years, more than has been suggested, and $44 billion in one year alone in 2035-36.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor has announced a plan to index tax brackets if elected.

item.title: Sick of maintaining your lawn? Transform it into a native garden
item.description: Many of us are looking to create a native garden as a lower maintenance, more sustainable and often cheaper alternative.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Scott

media:group.media:description: Think about a mix of medicinal, edible, toolmaking and visually appealing native plants instead of lawn.

item.title: ABC boss Hugh Marks flags TV cuts as broadcaster pivots to new platforms
item.description: The ABC's managing director says the public broadcaster's biggest challenge is "stopping things" as he seeks to grow audiences on new platforms without increasing staff.
item.dc:creator: David Taylor and Michael Janda

media:group.media:description: Hugh Marks says the ABC has regained the trust of its audience.
